rt1-9-2.title: Hands-on: set up a clean single-wall shot
rt1-9-2.intro: Single-wall is the first-choice technique because its geometry is clean and Ug is controlled with the full SFD working for you. Stretch the film gap to watch a far wall blur into uselessness — the DWSI effect — then pull the source back to meet the Ug spec and place the IQI on the source side, where single-wall viewing demands it.

tasks.label: Bring Ug within the 0.5 mm spec
tasks.hint: Ug = f x OFD / SOD — pull the source back or close the film gap; single-wall lets you do both.

tasks.label: Place the wire IQI on the source-side weld surface
tasks.hint: Single-wall viewing puts the IQI on the side facing the source — film-side placement is rejected.

rt1-9-3.title: Hands-on: time a panoramic gamma exposure
rt1-9-3.intro: A panoramic shot exposes every film around the girth weld in one firing, so the exposure time must be right the first time — there is no per-film re-shoot. Land the density window with the 60 Ci Ir-192 crawler source, then re-time the same weld 30 days of decay later, the round-to-round correction every pipeline crew makes.

tasks.label: Land film density in the 2.0-3.0 window
tasks.hint: Density follows exposure E = activity x time — every cassette around the circumference gets this one shot.

tasks.label: Re-shoot 30 days later at the same density
tasks.hint: After 30 days Ir-192 keeps about 75% of its activity — stretch the time so A x t matches the first shot.

rt1-9-4.title: Hands-on: protect the fluoroscopist with T-D-S
rt1-9-4.intro: Direct-screen viewing parks a person closer to an energized beam than anything else in radiography, so protection rests on time, distance, and shielding. Work all three levers: step back like a remote-viewing setup, stack shielding like the lead-glass window, and plan a limited viewing session under the dose cap.

tasks.label: Double your distance from the source — rate falls 4x
tasks.hint: Inverse square is why remote-screen viewing is safer: the operator reads a monitor in another room.

tasks.label: Stack shielding until the door post reads under 2 mR/h
tasks.hint: Each slab is one half-value layer — the lead-glass viewing window protects the same way, halving per HVL.

tasks.label: Plan a 15-minute viewing session under 25 mrem
tasks.hint: Dose = rate x hours — viewing periods are limited and alternated, so cut the rate until 15 minutes fits.
